content.full_text:
TO: [ ] JEANNE   [ ] JAY   FROM: Rich
[ ] LYNN   [ ] BARBARA   DATE: 2/25/81
[ ] RICH   [ ] BOB S.
[ ] HELEN   [ ] OTHER__________   T-81-182

[X] TELEPHONE ADVICE - SEE BELOW
[ ] CORRESPONDENCE ADVICE - SEE ATTACHED DRAFT
(If you have any comments, notify advisor within 1 day)

FILE LOCATION:

INCLUDE IN ADVICE PACKAGE: YES [ ] NO [X]

CALLER Kirk Pesner
REPRESENTING Dobbs & Nielsen
PHONE NUMBER: Los Angeles

QUESTION: During the period in which a 'PAC' is making "late contributions to candidates and/or measures, is the 'PAC' required to report contributions of $1,000 it receives as "late contributions"?

ANSWER: No! A "late contribution" is any contribution made to or received by 1) a candidate; 2) a controlled committee or 3) a committee formed or existing primarily to support or oppose a candidate or measure.
